Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. More precise elevation data for GraphHopper routing engine 30 upvotes, 0 comments
  2. Two studies in compiler optimisations 70 upvotes, 5 comments
  3. Power consumption of Game Boy flash cartridges (2021) 30 upvotes, 1 comments
  4. Shell Tricks That Make Life Easier (and Save Your Sanity) 41 upvotes, 3 comments
  5. Quantization from the Ground Up 244 upvotes, 46 comments
  6. My astrophotography in the movie Project Hail Mary 849 upvotes, 197 comments
  7. The EU still wants to scan your private messages and photos 1067 upvotes, 278 comments
  8. Data is everywhere. The government is buying it without a warrant 30 upvotes, 3 comments
  9. Personal Encyclopedias 44 upvotes, 6 comments
  10. Running Tesla Model 3's computer on my desk using parts from crashed cars 591 upvotes, 179 comments
  11. FreeCAD v1.1 252 upvotes, 77 comments
  12. Show HN: A plain-text cognitive architecture for Claude Code 88 upvotes, 25 comments
  13. My DIY FPGA board can run Quake II 136 upvotes, 44 comments
  14. False claims in a widely-cited paper 257 upvotes, 100 comments
  15. Thoughts on slowing the fuck down 823 upvotes, 378 comments
  16. Earthquake scientists reveal how overplowing weakens soil at experimental farm 155 upvotes, 62 comments
  17. Rendering complex scripts in terminal and OSC 66 30 upvotes, 8 comments
  18. The truth that haunts the Ramones: 'They sold more T-shirts than records' 98 upvotes, 39 comments
  19. Apple randomly closes bug reports unless you "verify" the bug remains unfixed 383 upvotes, 216 comments
  20. 90% of Claude-linked output going to GitHub repos w <2 stars 269 upvotes, 158 comments
  21. Woman who never stopped updating her lost dog's chip reunites with him after 11y 170 upvotes, 99 comments
  22. ARC-AGI-3 375 upvotes, 253 comments
  23. Supreme Court Sides with Cox in Copyright Fight over Pirated Music 336 upvotes, 259 comments
  24. Show HN: Optio – Orchestrate AI coding agents in K8s to go from ticket to PR 48 upvotes, 28 comments
  25. "Disregard That" Attacks 73 upvotes, 53 comments
  26. Show HN: Robust LLM Extractor for Websites in TypeScript 33 upvotes, 20 comments
  27. Jury finds Meta liable in case over child sexual exploitation on its platforms 377 upvotes, 471 comments
  28. Do Architects Still Need to Draw? (2020) 16 upvotes, 13 comments
  29. What Came After the 486? 11 upvotes, 8 comments
  30. Ashby (YC W19) Is Hiring Engineers Who Make Product Decisions 0 upvotes, 0 comments

Last generated 4:30pm, 26 March 2026. Source here.
